user_query:
We're launching our site next week and our About page reads bloated and a bit buzzwordy. Please do a full edit that combines a line edit, structural edit, tone adjustment, and a cut to meet the target reduction.

Read these files:
- input/draft_about.md (the current draft)
- input/brand_voice.md (tone and voice guidance)
- input/edit_constraints.yaml (target cut %, required phrases, banned words, and required section headings)

What I need you to deliver:
1) Write the fully edited About page to output/edited_about.md.
   - Reduce length by the target percentage in input/edit_constraints.yaml while preserving meaning.
   - Keep the exact brand name and all required phrases from the YAML.
   - Avoid all banned words from the YAML.
   - Reorganize into clear sections using the required headings listed in the YAML (use them as H2 headings unless the YAML says otherwise).
   - Keep the voice confident, warm, and plain-spoken per input/brand_voice.md.

2) Write an edit summary to output/edit_summary.md using this exact structure:
- What changed: [3–5 bullet points of specific, high-level edits and why you made them]
- What was preserved: [what you intentionally kept]
- Optional next step: [one suggestion for further improvement]

Do not create any other files. Please make sure the edited page stands alone as a clean, publication-ready draft.
